topic.what: Agentic threat hunting from raw Windows event logs using SQL queries
topic.how: LLM agents iteratively query SQLite databases containing 75K-135K log records to identify malicious timestamps; performance measured by Coverage Score (fraction of narrative steps detected)
topic.why_matters: Current LLMs fail dramatically at open-ended evidence-driven threat hunting despite strong security knowledge benchmark performance; establishes baseline for unsupervised SOC deployment readiness

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of coverable narrative steps detected per run
characteristics.context: Best model achieves 0.55 on 26 campaigns across 105 procedures; no model ever completes any hunt

characteristics.value: Best: 59% Defense Evasion; Worst: 0-4% Initial Access/Credential Access
characteristics.vs_baseline: Passing bar ≥50% per tactic; no model passes all 13
characteristics.context: Claude Opus 4.6 clears 7/13 tactics; GPT-5 has 2 complete blind spots

characteristics.what_it_measures: Correctly submitted malicious timestamps vs total ground truth
characteristics.vs_baseline: Best performer; flag-densest campaigns have 5,713-6,713 ground-truth flags
characteristics.context: On flag-densest campaigns Claude Opus 4.6 submits ~150 of 5,713+ flags (~2.6%)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Gap between observed flags (n_flags_in_query) and submitted flags (n_flags_in_submitted)
characteristics.value: 46 flags observed vs 113 submitted (Claude Opus 4.6 mean)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Indicates agents see evidence but fail to attribute
characteristics.context: Consistent pattern across models: observe more than they report

characteristics.what_itasures: LLM API cost in USD per hunt episode
characteristics.vs_baseline: Total 859 runs cost $1,672; GPT-5 costs $1.07 for 0.21 coverage
characteristics.context: Cost-performance Pareto shows Claude Opus 4.6 dominates coverage but at 17× GPT-5 cost

characteristics.vs_baseline: Claude Opus 4.6 explores deeper; GPT-5 commits earlier at turn 34
characteristics.context: 10-row result limit forces semantic compression; brute-force scanning infeasible
26 campaign seeds collectively cover 105 of 106 procedures

characteristics.vs_baseline: 500 synthetic benign records injected as noise
characteristics.context: 10-row query limit against 75K-135K records is information-retrieval problem

good_for: Evaluating LLM agents on agentic threat hunting tasks
Measuring SQL query authorship for security log analysis
Comparing model performance on MITRE ATT&CK tactic coverage
Benchmarking cost-performance tradeoffs in cyber defense agents
not_for: Single-step Q&A security tasks (open-ended hunting focus)
requires: Access to proprietary campaign data (26-seed release)
